title: pilosella paragoga subsp. phalacrodes
intro: Pilosella paragoga subsp. phalacrodes is a rare, endangered subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae), also known as a type of hawkweed. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of various health issues such as fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal problems. The plant is rich in flavonoids and sesquiterpenoids, which are believed to possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. Pilosella paragoga subsp. phalacrodes can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential side effects and the need for further research.
